name: Soviet Union (1955–1991)

colors.symbolism: the revolutionary struggle of the working class

description: A plain red field. In the upper hoist a gold hammer crossed by a sickle, surmounted by a gold-bordered red five-pointed star.
history: Adopted in its definitive 1955 statute form (the obverse-only design with no hammer-and-sickle on the reverse) by the Presidium of the Supreme Soviet on 19 August 1955. The basic red flag with hammer, sickle, and star was first established for the Soviet Union in 1923 and codified in the 1924 Constitution; the design was revised in detail in 1936, 1955, and 1980 (the latter dropping the reverse hammer-and-sickle entirely). Used until the dissolution of the USSR on 25 December 1991, when it was lowered from the Kremlin and replaced by the Russian tricolour.

features.symbolism: the blood of the workers and peasants shed in their struggle for liberation; the international communist movement
the alliance of industrial workers (hammer) and peasantry (sickle) under communism
the ultimate triumph of communism on the five inhabited continents; the Red Army
